items.title: Can ChatGPT Help You Hack a Website? Why You Still Need a WAF Like SafeLine
items.author: Sharon
items.content:
As AI tools like ChatGPT become more accessible and powerful, we’re witnessing a surprising side effect—AI-assisted vulnerability discovery. Prompts like “How do I bypass a login form using SQL injection?” or “What are common payloads to test for XSS?” are now not just hacker trivia—they're one prompt away.

So what happens when AI is at the fingertips of everyone, including malicious actors?

The answer is simple: You need stronger defense.

AI Is Making Attacks Easier—Not Harder

Large Language Models (LLMs) like ChatGPT can already:

  • Generate code to scan or crawl websites
  • Mimic browser behavior to bypass bot detection
  • Summarize responses to find vulnerabilities faster

And that’s without any black hat tuning. With tools like GPT-4, it doesn’t take a skilled hacker to launch serious reconnaissance or injection attempts.

This isn’t a scare tactic—it’s the new reality. Which brings us to .

Why SafeLine WAF Is Built for the AI Era

SafeLine isn't your typical rule-based WAF. It uses semantic-aware detection algorithms that don't rely on fragile signatures or static regex filters. Instead, it analyzes the behavior and intent of traffic—giving it a massive edge against automated tools, even those powered by AI.

Here’s what sets SafeLine apart:

  • SafeLine uses HTML/JS dynamic encryption to confuse automated scanners, making it hard for AI-powered crawlers to navigate.
  • It detects obfuscated payloads, deeply encoded attacks, and even unknown 0days—without needing manual rules.
  • SafeLine is completely open-source and free to use. No hidden features, no site limits.

ChatGPT May Write the Exploit—SafeLine Stops It

We tested SafeLine against popular AI-generated payloads for SQL injection, XSS, and file inclusion. The result?

✅ Over 98% of attacks were blockedfalse positive rate below 0.1%

✅ No need to configure complex rules manually

SafeLine’s intelligent engine doesn’t just block known threats—it spots patterns of malicious behavior even if the payload is new or AI-generated.

Built for Developers. Loved by Security Teams.

SafeLine is a perfect fit for:

  • Personal and indie projects – One-command setup, low resource usage
  • – High-performance WAF without the enterprise price
  • – Full visibility into detection logic and logs
  • – Built on Nginx, fully transparent, and extensible

Whether you're building the next SaaS platform or running a personal blog, SafeLine gives you AI-resilient protection—without needing a SOC team.

Let’s face it: AI isn’t going away. But neither are we.

items.title: Gurgaon vs Bengaluru: Where Are the Best AI Companies in 2025?
items.author: Priyanka Pandey
items.content:
In 2025, India stands at the forefront of the global AI revolution, with Gurgaon and Bengaluru emerging as pivotal hubs. While Bengaluru, often dubbed the 'Silicon Valley of India,' has a longstanding reputation in the tech industry, Gurgaon has rapidly ascended, attracting a plethora of AI startups and enterprises.

This article delves into the strengths of each city, spotlighting their and assessing their contributions to the AI landscape.

Gurgaon's AI Landscape in 2025

Gurgaon, strategically located near the national capital, has transformed into a vibrant tech corridor. Its proximity to major industries and a conducive business environment have fostered the growth of numerous AI companies.

Notable AI Companies in Gurgaon:

• Specializes in AI-driven solutions for governance and cultural analytics. • Offers AI-based video analytics and facial recognition technologies. • Focuses on AI-powered robotics for warehouse automation. • Provides AI tools for digital marketing optimization. • Utilizes AI for predictive analytics in the food and beverage industry.

These companies exemplify Gurgaon's diverse AI applications, ranging from industrial automation to consumer analytics.

Bengaluru's AI Ecosystem in 2025

Bengaluru has long been synonymous with technological innovation. Its robust infrastructure, academic institutions, and a culture of entrepreneurship have made it a magnet for AI enterprises.DEV Community

Leading AI Companies in Bengaluru:

• Happiest Minds Technologies: Delivers AI solutions across various sectors, including healthcare and finance. • Specializes in data analytics and decision sciences. • Operates a significant R&D center focusing on AI and deep learning. • Provides enterprise AI platforms for automated machine learning. • Offers AI-driven insights for Fortune 500 companies.

Bengaluru's AI firms are known for their advanced research and global clientele, reinforcing the city's status as an AI powerhouse.

Both Gurgaon and Bengaluru have carved distinct niches in India's AI landscape by 2025. Gurgaon's rapid ascent is marked by its diverse AI applications and strategic location, making it a hotspot for emerging AI ventures. Conversely, Bengaluru's deep-rooted tech ecosystem and emphasis on research continue to attract global AI leaders. The choice between the two cities hinges on specific business needs, be it Gurgaon's dynamic growth or Bengaluru's established expertise.

Q1: Which city has more AI startups in 2025, Gurgaon or Bengaluru?Bengaluru hosts a larger number of AI startups, attributed to its mature tech ecosystem and longstanding industry presence.Clutch

Q2: Are there specific AI sectors where Gurgaon leads over Bengaluru?Gurgaon has shown significant advancements in AI applications related to governance, logistics, and retail analytics.

Q3: How does the talent pool compare between the two cities? Bengaluru benefits from institutions like IISc and a vast tech workforce, while Gurgaon attracts talent from the NCR region and neighboring states.

Q4: Which city offers better infrastructure for AI companies? Bengaluru has a well-established IT infrastructure, though it faces urban challenges. Gurgaon boasts modern business parks and proximity to Delhi's resources.

Q5: Is government support for AI initiatives stronger in Gurgaon or Bengaluru? Both cities receive substantial government support, with Gurgaon benefiting from initiatives aimed at developing the NCR as a tech hub, and Bengaluru continuing to be a focal point for national tech policies.

items.title: When should I use LLMs?
items.author: Filip Trivan
items.content:
As LLMs like ChatGPT, Gemini, and Grok soar in popularity, the Spiderly library stands out by addressing what they can’t yet fully handle: project architecture that demands a broader perspective and deeper understanding. Developers leaning heavily on LLMs without grasping the generated code are more prone to errors that, if they slip into production, take longer to fix. Spiderly minimizes those risks.

With tool you create a solid foundation with whole app structure, email auth, Google auth, logging etc. Then, the Entity Administration Source Generator builds everything you need for entity management. From there, you focus your brainpower on solving the end user’s specific problems.

The star of Spiderly is its Entity Administration Source Generator (Spiderly.SourceGenerators project), a standalone tool. It delivers:

  1. Easily customizable, responsive admin forms for entities on the client side

  2. Client-side form validations and server-side object validations for incoming client data

  3. Controller methods for both client and server

  4. Flexible authorization methods

  5. Server-side CRUD methods for entity administration

  6. Methods for filtering and working with tables

Spiderly also tackles other essentials: authentication (client and server, using JWT tokens), API request rate limiting, logging, entity versioning, consistent database table naming, and client-side app layout design.

I’d love for this, open-source project to spark a community that sets a standard for solving general coding challenges worth tackling broadly. Right now, Spiderly focuses on web apps using C#, Azure, EF Core (Lazy Loading), Serilog, JWT, FluentValidation, Mapster, SQL Server, Angular, and PrimeNG.

Let’s build something great together!

items.title: A Better Yahoo Finance API Alternative for Developers in 2025
items.author: DollarPixel
items.content:
For years, developers have relied on Yahoo Finance for quick access to market data. It was free, easy to use, and worked well for prototypes. I’ve used it myself in side projects, backtesting tools, and even dashboards.

But let’s be honest — isn’t what it used to be. It’s unofficial, unstable, and limited in scope. At some point, you realize it’s time to move on.

That’s when I started looking for a better option. Something reliable. Something real-time. Something that works across markets — not just U.S. stocks.

I eventually landed on , and I think it’s worth sharing why it has become my go-to alternative.

Why Yahoo Finance Isn’t Enough Anymore

First, the obvious: Yahoo’s public API has long been shut down. What you’re using today — if you’re still using it — is a workaround. It can break anytime. And even when it works, you’re limited to delayed quotes and U.S. stock data.

There’s no real support. No guarantees. And certainly not something I’d build a production system on.

What Developers Actually Need

If you're building any serious trading tool, portfolio tracker, or signal engine, here’s what you probably care about:

  • Real-time market data (not 15 minutes delayed)
  • Support for multiple markets: not just U.S. stocks, but also China A-shares, Hong Kong stocks, forex, crypto, and maybe even commodities
  • Reliable and fast APIs — ideally both HTTP and WebSocket
  • Clear docs and easy integration
  • Commercial usage without worrying about licensing
  • Yahoo can’t do that. That’s where AllTick comes in.

AllTick is a real-time market data provider designed for developers. It gives you fast, stable access to financial markets across the globe.

Here’s what it covers: China A-shares

    You can query data using HTTP RESTful API, or set up WebSocket streams for real-time updates. It’s straightforward to integrate, and the latency is low enough for serious use cases — like algo trading or high-frequency analytics.

    One thing to note: AllTick focuses on market quotes. You won’t find financial statements or fundamentals here — it’s purely market data. That’s exactly what I wanted, so no complaints.

    AllTick vs Yahoo Finance API — A Quick Look

    To give you an idea, here’s how I’ve been using AllTick:

    • Real-time stock price alerts via WebSocket
    • Forex market dashboard with streaming quotes
    • Backtesting strategies on U.S. and Hong Kong stocks using tick data

    Setting up the API was quick. The docs are clear. You get an API key, make a few HTTP calls, and you’re in.

    Here’s a super basic example in Python:

    import time

import requests # pip3 install requests
import json

# Extra headers
test_headers = {
    'Content-Type' : 'application/json'
}

'''
Encode the following JSON with URL encoding and copy it to the "query" field in the query string of an HTTP request.
{"trace" : "python_http_test1","data" : {"code" : "700.HK","kline_type" : 1,"kline_timestamp_end" : 0,"query_kline_num" : 2,"adjust_type": 0}}
{"trace" : "python_http_test2","data" : {"symbol_list": [{"code": "700.HK"},{"code": "UNH.US"}]}}
{"trace" : "python_http_test3","data" : {"symbol_list": [{"code": "700.HK"},{"code": "UNH.US"}]}}
'''
test_url1 = 'http://quote.aatest.online/quote-stock-b-api/kline?token=e945d7d9-9e6e-4721-922a-7251a9d311d0-1678159756806&query=%7B%22trace%22%20%3A%20%22python_http_test1%22%2C%22data%22%20%3A%20%7B%22code%22%20%3A%20%22700.HK%22%2C%22kline_type%22%20%3A%201%2C%22kline_timestamp_end%22%20%3A%200%2C%22query_kline_num%22%20%3A%202%2C%22adjust_type%22%3A%200%7D%7D'
test_url2 = 'http://quote.aatest.online/quote-stock-b-api/depth-tick?token=e945d7d9-9e6e-4721-922a-7251a9d311d0-1678159756806&query=%7B%22trace%22%20%3A%20%22python_http_test2%22%2C%22data%22%20%3A%20%7B%22symbol_list%22%3A%20%5B%7B%22code%22%3A%20%22700.HK%22%7D%2C%7B%22code%22%3A%20%22UNH.US%22%7D%5D%7D%7D'
test_url3 = 'http://quote.aatest.online/quote-stock-b-api/trade-tick?token=e945d7d9-9e6e-4721-922a-7251a9d311d0-1678159756806&query=%7B%22trace%22%20%3A%20%22python_http_test3%22%2C%22data%22%20%3A%20%7B%22symbol_list%22%3A%20%5B%7B%22code%22%3A%20%22700.HK%22%7D%2C%7B%22code%22%3A%20%22UNH.US%22%7D%5D%7D%7D'

resp1 = requests.get(url=test_url1, headers=test_headers)
time.sleep(1)
resp2 = requests.get(url=test_url2, headers=test_headers)
time.sleep(1)
resp3 = requests.get(url=test_url3, headers=test_headers)

# Decoded text returned by the request
text1 = resp1.text
print(text1)

text2 = resp2.text
print(text2)

text3 = resp3.text
print(text3)

    And if you’re into WebSockets:

    import json
import websocket    # pip install websocket-client

class Feed(object):

    def __init__(self):
        self.url = 'wss://quote.aatest.online/quote-stock-b-ws-api?token=e945d7d9-9e6e-4721-922a-7251a9d311d0-1678159756806'
        self.ws = None

    def on_open(self, ws):
        """
        Callback object which is called at opening websocket.
        1 argument:
        @ ws: the WebSocketApp object
        """
        print('A new WebSocketApp is opened!')

        sub_param = {
            "cmd_id": 22002, 
            "seq_id": 123,
            "trace":"3baaa938-f92c-4a74-a228-fd49d5e2f8bc-1678419657806",
            "data":{
                "symbol_list":[
                    {
                        "code": "700.HK",
                        "depth_level": 5,
                    },
                    {
                        "code": "UNH.US",
                        "depth_level": 5,
                    }
                ]
            }
        }

        sub_str = json.dumps(sub_param)
        ws.send(sub_str)
        print("depth quote are subscribed!")

    def on_data(self, ws, string, type, continue_flag):
        """
        4 argument.
        The 1st argument is this class object.
        The 2nd argument is utf-8 string which we get from the server.
        The 3rd argument is data type. ABNF.OPCODE_TEXT or ABNF.OPCODE_BINARY will be came.
        The 4th argument is continue flag. If 0, the data continue
        """

    def on_message(self, ws, message):
        """
        Callback object which is called when received data.
        2 arguments:
        @ ws: the WebSocketApp object
        @ message: utf-8 data received from the server
        """

        result = eval(message)
        print(result)

    def on_error(self, ws, error):
        """
        Callback object which is called when got an error.
        2 arguments:
        @ ws: the WebSocketApp object
        @ error: exception object
        """
        print(error)

    def on_close(self, ws, close_status_code, close_msg):
        """
        Callback object which is called when the connection is closed.
        2 arguments:
        @ ws: the WebSocketApp object
        @ close_status_code
        @ close_msg
        """
        print('The connection is closed!')

    def start(self):
        self.ws = websocket.WebSocketApp(
            self.url,
            on_open=self.on_open,
            on_message=self.on_message,
            on_data=self.on_data,
            on_error=self.on_error,
            on_close=self.on_close,
        )
        self.ws.run_forever()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    feed = Feed()
    feed.start()

    You can sign up and get an API key right away. There’s a free trial tier if you just want to try it out. The docs cover everything — HTTP endpoints, WebSocket structure, market codes, etc.

    They also support Python and JavaScript pretty well, so you won’t be fighting the API like some of the older data providers.

    If you’ve outgrown Yahoo Finance — or just want something faster, more stable, and developer-friendly — give AllTick a try.

    It won’t give you financial statements or dividend history, but for real-time quotes across multiple markets, it’s a solid upgrade.

    And most importantly, it feels like an API built for developers, not just something bolted onto a website.

items.title: Agentic AI Explained: Transforming Industries with Autonomous Intelligence
items.author: Eastern Peak
items.content:
Today, artificial intelligence (AI) is everywhere—it’s successfully integrated into healthcare, HR, content creation, and everything beyond. This technology is here to stay, and more companies are embracing this shift.

The numbers for AI exceed almost every other technology out there: the market size for AI in 2025 is projected to grow to $243.72 billion, with a CAGR of nearly 28% from 2025 to 2030. It’s probably unsurprising, then, that AI doesn’t stay in place and continues developing. While earlier models were relatively limited and required significant human input, today’s AI is adaptable and leverages deep learning and neural networks to grow more creative and solve diverse challenges.

Now, a new step in AI evolution is here—agentic AI. Not limited by the constraints of traditional AI, agentic AI operates with autonomy, continuously learning, making decisions, and taking actions with minimal human intervention.

The global market demand for agentic AI tools will jump: In 2024, its value was around $5.1 billion, but in 2030, it’s expected to reach $47 billion. By combining the new technological freedom and ability to learn with the experiences of the previous models, agentic AI will revolutionize all industries.

This article will discuss agentic AI definition and agentic AI vs. traditional AI, provide agentic AI use cases, and answer the question “What are the potential risks associated with agentic AI?”

Let’s start with the easy stuff: what does agentic AI mean? You are already familiar with what AI does—it usually analyzes data and generates content based on the input. However, agentic AI is different. Agentic AI is a system that operates with a high level of autonomy and primarily targets problem-solving, decision-making, and developing new solutions to complex challenges.

The key features of agentic AI are:

  • autonomy in decision-making;
  • usage of large language models (LLMs), machine learning (ML), and natural language processing (NLP);
  • better contextual understanding;
  • limited human involvement and
  • high level of integration with external systems.

Now that this is clear, how does agentic AI differ from traditional AI systems?

Traditional AI, already effective enough, significantly relies on input to continue making decisions and growing. However, agentic AI is proactive and uses goal-oriented reasoning to set specific tasks and adjust based on the context of the situation. This leads to another difference: traditional AI is limited by specific scenarios, while agentic AI understands context and changes its behavior in real time.

How these types of AI handle tasks also differs: the traditional model is more single-task-focused. In contrast, the agentic one can handle multi-level issues and involve complex problem-solving. Both AIs can learn, but traditional AI uses mostly past situations to refine its further actions—it’s different for agentic AI that continuously refines strategies and can predict situations that haven’t yet occurred.

Such a shift illustrates the rapid advancement of how machines interact with the world and their role in improving people’s lives.

Agentic AI use cases in different industries

What industries are most likely to benefit from agentic AI? From self-driving vehicles to intelligent customer service assistants, these agentic AI solutions assist humans in the most demanding environments.

Agentic AI in transportation

Most people can easily envision agentic AI in transportation because it’s been around for some time. You’ve heard about autonomous vehicles: agentic AI enables them to actively perceive their surroundings (traffic, road situation, weather conditions, etc.) and make split-second decisions.

These AI-driven systems process real-time sensor data from cameras, LiDAR, and radar to detect and understand the physical environment, immediately updating if something changes.

The autonomous vehicle market share in 2022 was almost $105 billion and is expected to reach nearly $615 billion in 2030. With agentic AI, traditional self-driving vehicles don’t just rely on rules but continuously adapt to new driving conditions, which improves safety on the streets.

Have you heard of the robots or programs that help doctors perform operations or diagnose illnesses? That’s how helpful agentic AI in healthcare can be, and the opportunities are growing every day. Agentic AI enhances diagnostics by analyzing vast medical datasets, detecting patterns that human doctors might miss, and continuously refining its accuracy.

Just last year’s research publication has found that AI has outstanding accuracy rates, even achieving up to 92.5% performance success in some risk assessments for different health conditions.

In surgeries, AI assistants can perform the precise movements that can be too difficult for human agents due to simple limitations of their bodies. For example, mistakes from overworking, stress, or even shaking hands can be finally minimized.

The finance market, from trading to banking, is lush for AI integrations—this technology allows companies and individuals to make smarter decisions and increase their wealth. Even more so, they grow increasingly helpful for fraud prevention and security measures.

Unlike rule-based fraud detection systems, agentic AI learns from evolving fraud patterns, adapting its detection methods in real time to counter emerging threats. Trading, on the other hand, gets easier as this technology makes decisions based on data-driven strategies.

Statistics reveal that AI in finance is actively used today: The AI market size in fintech is $42.83 billion worldwide, and as it gets easier to integrate, it will take up more space and offer new solutions.

Agentic AI in manufacturing and supply chain management

There are plenty of opportunities for agentic AI in manufacturing: certain areas require better performance by adjusting workflow, predicting equipment failures, and optimizing resource allocation.

While the data on agentic AI remains limited due to how novel this AI branch is, there is valuable information on how this technology is generally used. In 2020, quality control had the most use cases for it (59%) in the manufacturing industry, with inventory management (44%) and monitoring/diagnostics (32%) following it.

In the supply chain, agentic AI can assist professionals with inventory tracking and demand forecasting, reducing expenditures on potential prediction errors.

Logistics is one of the industries where the opportunities of agentic AI can be useful on all levels. In 2024, AI in logistics reached almost $18 billion and is expected to grow to $26.35 billion.

With agentic AI, this technology can help with smart routing and 24/7 shipment tracking. As a result, companies can minimize losses due to delays or wrong delivery. In warehouses, autonomous robots can manage inventory and sort packages.

Smart cities and infrastructure

Urban environments are experiencing a rapid transformation, with traditional views on how cities should look becoming redundant. Today, urban planning is more than just creating skyscrapers—it’s about making green areas and inclusive spaces for all.

Agentic AI can contribute to this by improving energy consumption, planning building allocation, and enhancing public safety, cleanliness of air/water, and more. As cities become more connected as systems, they can provide information about traffic, accidents, or even infrastructure maintenance needs.

Customer service already readily utilizes AI to handle routine tasks and respond to repetitive inquiries. In 2024, the most popular use cases of AI in contact centers were voice authentication and process automation (both 37%).

However, as agentic AI can handle more complex tasks, customer service will likely provide more services and opportunities. It will handle complex interactions or predict customer behavior during interactions. This change will shift repetitive and (sometimes) frustrating interactions toward more natural and practical solutions.

These agentic AI applications reveal how diverse they can be. However, what industries are currently benefiting the most from agentic AI? Healthcare, logistics, and manufacturing are the most likely answers.

Examples of agentic AI in action

For those still asking, “How does agentic AI differ from traditional AI?” the case studies below can provide impressive examples of the newest technology adaptation.

Ema—agentic AI in customer support

Ema brings the latest experiences of agentic AI into its tools. It offers an AI chatbot, agent assistant, agent QA, and knowledge base assistant. This agentic AI example claims to resolve up to 70% of customer issues with its AI bot and save over 80% of agent time with its agent assistant. It’s an adaptable service that moves from responding to inputs and can be used as a tool for collaboration with consultants.

Juna.ai—an agentic process control platform

Juna.ai is a platform that uses agentic AI to control its clients’ industrial processes. Among all agentic AI examples in this list, it takes the top place for innovation. It tracks plant processes, optimizes production, and reduces energy consumption. It follows all the plant procedures and generates reports on demand. Its real-time updates with continuous improvement suggestions rely on agentic AI’s novel tech opportunities.

Atomwise—agentic AI in drug discovery

Atomwise uses agentic AI to discover drugs with the help of its technology, AtomNet. With contextual adaptation and fast learning, it studies chemical datasets to predict molecular behavior and potential drug performance. The company compares its technology to image recognition. The pharma industry currently taps into AI-powered molecular simulations and data analysis to develop new drugs or discover their inefficiencies.

Waymo has developed Waymo Driver, which leverages the latest agentic AI to offer a completely hands-off experience for passengers. You have probably heard of similar examples of agentic AI, but many of them aren’t really the same and rely on humans for control.

According to its website, Waymo emphasizes safety and has “millions of miles driven on public roads and billions of miles in simulation,” which has enabled the company to make an independent driver. It maps out the environment and keeps track of external and internal factors affecting the drive.

These agentic AI startups demonstrate how to respond to the already existing need and turn it into something unique.

Challenges and risks of agentic AI

Despite the undeniable potential of agentic AI, it’s not immune to threats and challenges, especially since it’s so new. Businesses planning to adopt it and asking themselves, “How to create agentic AI?” should be aware of the risks to ensure they know how to address them beforehand. So, the question you should ask instead is, “What are the main challenges in implementing agentic AI?”

Ethical concerns and bias

With AI, there’s always a danger of bias due to the datasets it’s trained on. If not properly addressed, these biases can lead to unfair outcomes in hiring or law enforcement, for instance, where they can hurt vulnerable populations.

What are the ethical considerations associated with agentic AI? It grows more autonomous and no longer requires human input—this will create questions of whether technology should be so freely making decisions that affect humans without much control.

Predictability and transparency

Unlike traditional AI, agentic AI dynamically learns and adapts, leading to a less predictable decision-making process. Such a fast agentic AI development and improvement makes it more difficult to control. This technology has not been studied entirely.

Many businesses still ask, “How does agentic AI work?” only after implementing it, which can cause problematic outcomes. It’s still not entirely clear how these AI technologies will perform in high-stakes situations, especially in healthcare, politics, finance, or criminal justice.

Plus, the lack of transparency makes AI auditing more complex, leading to a particular limitation of innovation management.

Agentic AI automation can be a target for security threats as it starts to handle sensitive and financially valuable data. If it’s accessed, it can wreak havoc on the business it attacks. There’s also a risk of error—even the best technologies can fail, which can lead to catastrophic results if they control critical infrastructure or financial markets.

Cybercriminals can also use the same technologies to attack private or public resources or integrate into the areas we have highlighted above.

The groundbreaking shift with agentic AI we are witnessing will revolutionize how we perceive the integration between humans and technology. Businesses that want to stay competitive should leverage it to optimize their performance and enhance problem-solving before they get left behind.

Need help determining how to build agentic AI or integrate it into your operations? Contact us. We’ve got experience with the latest industry trends and know how to make a unique product for your budget and vision.

items.title: MarketSense AI: Real-Time Market Intelligence Agent
items.author: Pranav jana
items.content:
I created , an intelligent agent that provides real-time market intelligence for investors, traders, and financial analysts. By continuously monitoring financial news, social media sentiment, and market data across multiple sources, MarketSense AI delivers actionable insights that help users make informed investment decisions.

Unlike traditional market analysis tools that rely on static or delayed data, MarketSense AI leverages Bright Data's infrastructure to access real-time information from across the web, ensuring users have the most current data available when making critical financial decisions.

  • Real-Time Financial News Aggregation: Continuously monitors and extracts relevant information from major financial news sources, blogs, and market updates.
  • Social Media Sentiment Analysis: Tracks discussions about stocks, cryptocurrencies, and market trends across Twitter, Reddit, and specialized investment forums.
  • : Pulls real-time price data, trading volume, and technical indicators for stocks, cryptocurrencies, and other financial instruments.
  • Natural Language Insights: Converts complex market data into clear, actionable insights through conversational AI.
  • : Notifies users of significant market movements, news, or sentiment shifts relevant to their portfolio.
  1. Users create a profile specifying their investment interests (stocks, cryptocurrencies, sectors)
  2. MarketSense AI continuously monitors multiple data sources for relevant information
  3. The platform processes and analyzes the data using machine learning algorithms
  4. Users receive personalized insights through the dashboard or notification system
  5. The AI learns from user feedback to improve future recommendations

How I Used Bright Data's Infrastructure

MarketSense AI relies heavily on Bright Data's MCP server to perform all four key actions:

I leveraged Bright Data's web unlocker to discover relevant financial content across diverse sources that would otherwise be inaccessible:

  • Financial news sites with paywalls (WSJ, Financial Times, Bloomberg)
  • Investment forums with complex authentication requirements
  • Specialized financial data providers with rate limiting
  • Social media platforms with API restrictions

MarketSense AI leverages Bright Data's capabilities to access:

  • Dynamic JavaScript-rendered financial dashboards
  • Financial sites with sophisticated bot protection
  • Login-protected investor forums and research portals
  • Financial data terminals with complex UI

The system extracts structured, real-time financial data at scale from:

  • Stock price feeds and market indices
  • Company financial statements
  • Analyst reports and predictions
  • Social sentiment indicators

MarketSense AI interacts with web interfaces just as a human would:

  • Navigating complex financial dashboards
  • Clicking through multi-page reports
  • Adjusting filters and parameters on data visualizations
  • Downloading CSV reports and data exports

By leveraging Bright Data's real-time web access capabilities, MarketSense AI significantly outperforms traditional market intelligence solutions:

Traditional financial data platforms typically deliver market information with a 15-20 minute delay, while news aggregation can take hours. With MarketSense AI powered by Bright Data:

  • Market data is refreshed in real-time (1-2 second intervals)
  • Breaking financial news is detected and processed within 8-12 seconds of publication
  • Social sentiment shifts are identified within 45 seconds (vs. hours with traditional monitoring)
  • Trading signals are generated 67% faster than conventional systems

Traditional approach: Limited to publicly accessible data or expensive subscription services. MarketSense AI: Comprehensive coverage across paywalled, social, and specialized sources.

  • Monitors 3.2x more financial news sources (783 vs. typical 245)
  • Tracks sentiment across 27 specialized investment communities (vs. 5-8 for conventional tools)
  • Analyzes 94% of publicly available analyst reports (vs. 62% industry average)
  • Processes data from 47 international markets in real-time (vs. 12-15 for standard platforms)

Traditional approach: Often relies on incomplete data and misses crucial context from diverse sources. MarketSense AI: Combines data from hundreds of sources to provide a holistic market view.

  • 89% more accurate sentiment analysis (benchmarked against retrospective analysis)
  • 78% reduction in false trading signals
  • 37% increase in correlation between predictions and actual market movements
  • 64% improvement in identifying relevant news vs. noise

These performance improvements translate to tangible business value:

  • 32% average improvement in portfolio performance in backtesting scenarios
  • 27% reduction in research time for financial analysts
  • 44% increase in relevant actionable insights per day
  • 53% faster response to market-moving events

MarketSense AI is built on a modern tech stack:

The system consists of five primary components:

  1. Data Collection Layer (powered by Bright Data)
  2. Processing and Analysis Engine
  3. Machine Learning Pipeline
  4. API Gateway and Backend Services
  5. Frontend Dashboard Application

The frontend is built with Next.js and Tailwind CSS, providing a responsive and intuitive interface:

  • : Built with React and Chart.js for real-time data visualization
  • : Redux state management for personalized settings
  • : WebSocket integration for real-time alerts
  • : Mobile-optimized views for on-the-go market monitoring
  • : JWT-based auth flow with role-based access control

The backend architecture uses Node.js with Express, organized as microservices:

  • : Routes requests to appropriate microservices
  • : Combines data from multiple sources
  • : Processes financial data for insights
  • : Monitors for user-defined conditions
  • Bright Data Integration Service: Manages web data collection
  • : Handles user management and security

Data Storage and Management

The data architecture uses a hybrid approach for optimal performance:

  • : Primary storage for time-series financial data
  • : Relational data, including user profiles and settings
  • : Caching layer for frequent queries and real-time updates
  • : Full-text search for news and unstructured content
  • : Historical data archiving

AI and Machine Learning Components

MarketSense AI leverages several ML models for financial intelligence:

  • : Fine-tuned Mistral model for financial text
  • : Ensemble of gradient boosting and LSTM networks
  • : Isolation Forest algorithm for unusual market events
  • : Identifies relationships between different financial signals
  • : Orchestrates AI components for insight generation

Deployment and Infrastructure

The system is deployed across multiple cloud services for reliability:

  • : Vercel for Next.js hosting and global CDN
  • : AWS ECS with auto-scaling
  • : Managed PostgreSQL with TimescaleDB extension
  • : Combination of AWS SageMaker and custom containers
  • : Datadog for performance metrics and alerting
  • : GitHub Actions for automated testing and deployment

I'm continuing to enhance MarketSense AI with:

  1. Integration with additional financial data sources
  2. Enhanced AI-driven predictive analytics
  3. Portfolio optimization recommendations
  4. Mobile app with push notifications for critical alerts
  5. API access for integration with trading platforms

MarketSense AI demonstrates how Bright Data's infrastructure can power sophisticated AI agents that deliver real business value. By providing instant access to comprehensive financial intelligence, MarketSense AI helps users make smarter investment decisions based on current information.

The project showcases Bright Data's MCP server's four key capabilities: discovering relevant content across the web, accessing even protected sites, extracting structured data at scale, and interacting with complex web interfaces just as a human would.

items.title: The Importance of Digital Marketing Courses in Today’s Job Market
items.author: Ampro TechValley
items.content:
In a world where digital presence defines business success, the demand for skilled digital marketers has skyrocketed. From multinational corporations to small local businesses, everyone is turning to digital platforms to reach, engage, and convert customers. This shift has not only redefined marketing but also opened up vast opportunities for professionals equipped with digital skills.

Digital marketing is more than just social media posts or online ads—it’s a strategic combination of search engine optimization (SEO), content creation, email marketing, analytics, paid advertising, and more. As companies increasingly focus on online customer journeys, the need for professionals who understand the nuances of digital engagement has grown exponentially.

Versatility Across Industries Digital marketing skills are applicable across virtually every industry. Healthcare providers use content and SEO to educate patients, real estate firms rely on social media for lead generation, and e-commerce businesses depend on paid advertising for conversions. This versatility means professionals from diverse educational backgrounds—be it IT, business, design, or even arts—can transition into digital marketing roles with the right training.

Career Growth and Flexibility One of the most appealing aspects of digital marketing is the flexibility it offers. Whether working in-house, for an agency, or as a freelancer, digital marketers enjoy the ability to work remotely and adapt to different industries. Additionally, the career path is dynamic. With experience, professionals can move into roles like Digital Marketing Manager, SEO Specialist, Content Strategist, or even Chief Marketing Officer (CMO).

Investing in a digital marketing course is often more cost-effective than traditional education, with a quicker return on investment. Most courses offer certifications that are recognized by employers, giving candidates a competitive edge in the job market. These programs also tend to include real-world projects and case studies, making the learning experience practical and relevant.

For those looking to enter this field, a Digital Marketing Course in Salem can offer localized training that’s aligned with regional job markets, while still preparing students for global digital roles.

The job market is evolving, and digital fluency is becoming essential. Whether you’re a recent graduate, a professional looking to upskill, or an entrepreneur aiming to expand your business online, a strong foundation in digital marketing can open doors to countless opportunities. With the right course, commitment, and creativity, you can carve a rewarding career in one of the fastest-growing sectors of the modern economy.

items.title: Why Every Business Needs an AI Chatbot in 2025
items.author: Jack Samuel
items.content:
As we move further into the digital age, the expectations of customers are rapidly changing. In 2025, speed, personalization, and 24/7 availability aren't just added bonuses they're baseline requirements. One of the most effective ways businesses are keeping up with these demands is through AI chatbots. From automating customer support to boosting sales and delivering personalized experiences, AI chatbots have become essential. Whether you're a startup or a large enterprise, integrating a chatbot into your operations is no longer optional it's strategic. And with the help of an expert AI chatbot development company, businesses across industries can stay ahead of the curve. Let’s explore why every business needs an AI chatbot in 2025, and how this technology is shaping the future.

  1. Customers Demand Instant and 24/7 Support Your customers don’t want to wait. In fact, long wait times are one of the biggest reasons for customer dissatisfaction. In 2025, users expect real-time assistance any time of day.

    That’s where AI chatbot development comes in. These bots provide round-the-clock support, answer frequently asked questions, and resolve common issues without human intervention. This ensures a smooth customer experience, even outside of traditional business hours.

    Whether someone is shopping online at midnight or needs help with a banking query at dawn, your AI chatbot will be there to assist.

  2. Cost-Efficient and Scalable Support Hiring and training a full customer support team can be expensive especially as your business grows. AI chatbots help reduce operational costs by handling repetitive and time-consuming tasks automatically.

A single chatbot can: Answer hundreds of inquiries simultaneously

Route complex issues to human agents

Handle multilingual communication

Provide consistent and error-free responses

Investing in quality AI chatbot development allows your business to scale support without scaling costs.

  1. Smart Sales Assistant and Lead Generator Today’s AI chatbots go beyond just answering questions. With the right design and integration, they can act as virtual sales assistants, guiding visitors through your website, recommending products, collecting user data, and encouraging purchases.

    Businesses that leverage AI chatbot development for lead generation and conversion are seeing higher ROI and customer engagement. In fact, many e-commerce and SaaS companies report a significant uptick in leads just by adding a well-trained chatbot.

  2. Valuable Customer Insights and Analytics Every conversation with a chatbot is a chance to learn about your customers. AI chatbots collect data on user behavior, preferences, common questions, and more. This information helps you make data-driven decisions to improve products, services, and customer journeys.

    Through advanced analytics dashboards, you’ll know what’s working, where customers are dropping off, and how to improve their experience. This feedback loop is critical for business growth in 2025.

  3. AI in Healthcare and Beyond AI chatbots aren’t limited to retail and customer service. AI in healthcare is a growing field where chatbots play a vital role in supporting patients and healthcare providers. From appointment scheduling to symptom checking and follow-ups, healthcare AI chatbots help streamline services, reduce administrative load, and improve patient access to care. An experienced AI chatbot development company can help build HIPAA-compliant, secure bots tailored for healthcare needs, making patient care more accessible and efficient.

    This trend is only going to grow as the need for faster, safer, and more connected healthcare services increases in 2025 and beyond.

  4. Seamless Integration with Your Ecosystem Today’s AI chatbots are fully integrable with popular business tools such as:

CRMs (like Salesforce or HubSpot)

E-commerce platforms (Shopify, WooCommerce)

Communication tools (Slack, Microsoft Teams)

Payment gateways and booking systems

With these integrations, chatbots become more than just a support tool they become a core part of your business operations. A skilled AI chatbot development company can ensure smooth integration with your systems, helping you automate workflows and deliver a seamless customer experience.

  1. Personalized Customer Experiences Modern customers want experiences tailored to their needs. AI chatbots now use data, previous interactions, and browsing behavior to deliver personalized recommendations and responses.

    This type of interaction builds trust, improves user satisfaction, and increases repeat business. Thanks to machine learning and

    NLP (Natural Language Processing), chatbots can now “understand” intent and sentiment, making every conversation feel more human.

  2. Stay Ahead of the Competition Your competitors are already adopting AI chatbots or planning to. The businesses that embrace AI chatbot development now will be better positioned to meet customer demands, reduce costs, and drive innovation.

    In industries like finance, travel, real estate, education, and healthcare, AI chatbots are becoming standard. Don’t be left behind in 2025 get ahead with a chatbot that aligns with your brand and business goals.

conclusion The future of business is conversational, intelligent, and AI-powered. Whether you want to improve support, boost sales, or offer personalized services, AI chatbots are a must-have in 2025.AI chatbot development company ensures you get a custom solution tailored to your industry, whether you're in e-commerce or exploring the possibilities of AI in healthcare.

items.title: Role Of AI in Customer Experience
items.author: Aelum Consulting
items.content:
AI-powered CX is a concept that uses AI technology such as chatbots, and digital agents to deliver fast, efficient, personalized, and proactive experiences at length. Activating AI for CX starts with automating manual tasks such as emails, appointment scheduling, customer queries, and manual data entry, leaving ample space for creative tasks that enhance customer engagement and strategy.

For example, Amazon uses generative AI to create personalized recommendations. Apple uses AI to streamline customer services, providing customized solutions and automating inquiries.

AI capabilities allow organizations to create more intuitive, meaningful, and personalized interactions, profoundly transforming customer experience (CX). Here are some of the key areas where AI is making a big difference:

AI simplifies complex processes by automating repetitive tasks, allowing teams to focus on more value-added activities. From ticket management to processing requests, AI tools save both time and effort.

2. Enhanced Agent Performance

AI-driven insights help agents work smarter, not harder. AI enables agents to resolve customer queries faster and more accurately by providing real-time suggestions, predictive analytics, and next-step guidance.

AI-powered chatbots and virtual assistants offer round-the-clock support, ensuring customers are never left waiting. They can handle multiple queries simultaneously, reducing response times and improving customer satisfaction.

4. Predicting Customer Behavior

AI uses data to anticipate what customers want, even before they ask. Businesses can offer personalized recommendations by analyzing past behavior and purchase patterns, creating a more engaging experience.

AI empowers customers to solve problems independently through smart self-service platforms. These tools answer frequently asked questions, troubleshoot issues, and guide users through processes without human intervention.

6. Onboarding New Customers Faster

AI can simplify and speed up onboarding, automatically collecting and verifying customer information. This reduces friction and ensures a smoother start for new clients.

7. Personalized Customer Interactions

AI allows companies to tailor every interaction to the individual. By leveraging data on customer preferences, behaviors, and history, businesses can offer more relevant and personalized support, enhancing loyalty and satisfaction.

Automating tasks like answering FAQs and processing orders reduces human error. AI ensures that responses are accurate, analyzes customer data quickly, and even detects mistakes in real-time, offering corrections during live interactions.

From managing customer inquiries to processing returns, AI simplifies processes across the board. This leads to more efficient operations, fewer delays, and a seamless customer experience.

Going Forward with AI-powered ServiceNow Customer Experience

Well, we understand your pain and want to help in any way we can, so we are here to address some of your top challenges. Ultimately, AI-powered CX is poised to become a game-changer for your organization, making customer interactions seamless.

items.title: Scaling Apache SeaTunnel for Enterprise: Billion-Level Data Processing and Intelligent Fault Tolerance in Real-World Use Cases
items.author: Apache SeaTunnel
items.content:
With the continuous complexity of data sources and the rapid evolution of business demands, general-purpose data integration frameworks often face many challenges in practical deployment: frequent issues such as irregular data structures, missing fields, mixed sensitive information, and unclear data semantics. To better address these complex scenarios, a leading publicly listed cybersecurity enterprise has performed secondary development based on Apache SeaTunnel, building a scalable, easy-to-maintain data processing and intelligent fault-tolerance mechanism suitable for complex scenarios. This article will comprehensively introduce the relevant technical implementations around actual functional extensions and design concepts.

Background and Pain Points

In practical business scenarios, the data sources we face are highly heterogeneous, including but not limited to log files, FTP/SFTP files, Kafka messages, and database changes. The data itself may be structurally inconsistent or even unstructured text or semi-structured XML format. The following problems are particularly prominent:

  • Insufficient complex data parsing capability: Unable to parse and ingest complex data such as XML, key-value, and irregular data.
  • Lack of data completion and dictionary translation capability: Unable to complete asset information when supplementing raw logs, resulting in incomplete data and missing key information, which leads to insufficient data analysis capability and inability to mine data value.
  • Limited file reading modes: Unable to capture and parse newly added logs in real time, causing delays in security threat detection and loss of real-time analysis and subsequent system alert value.
  • Weak exception handling mechanisms: During task execution, data senders may change logs without notifying receivers, causing task interruption. Without notification of log changes, it is difficult to quickly locate and solve problems.

New Features: Processing and Transformation Capability Extensions Based on SeaTunnel

To address the above complex scenarios, we built multiple Transform plugins based on SeaTunnel for regex transform, XML parsing, Key-Value parsing, dynamic data completion, IP address completion, data masking, dictionary translation, extended Incremental reading for SFTP/FTP, and other processing.

1. Regex Parsing (Regex Transform)

Used for parsing structured or semi-structured text fields. By configuring regular expressions and specifying group mappings, raw text can be split into multiple business fields. This method is widely used in log parsing and field splitting scenarios.

  • : The original field to be parsed
  • : Regular expression, e.g.,
  • : The mapping relationship between parsed result fields and regex capture group indexes

Using the VTD-XML parser combined with XPath expressions to precisely extract XML nodes, attributes, and text content, converting them into structured data.

Parse strings like "key1=value1;key2=value2" into structured fields. Supports configuration of key-value and field delimiters.

  • : Upstream key-value string field
  • : Key-value pair delimiter (e.g., )
  • : Key and value delimiter (e.g., )
  • : Set of target mapped field keys

4. Dynamic Data Completion (Lookup Enrichment)

Dynamically fill in missing fields using auxiliary data streams or dictionary tables, such as completing device asset information, user attributes, etc.

Source table join field, used to get the source table field value
dimension_table_join_fieldDimension table join field, used to get the dimension table data
JDBC URL path for the dimension table
SQL statement for the dimension table, the queried fields will be output to the next level process
data_cache_expire_time_minutesData cache refresh time in minutes

Implementation Highlights:

  • Support external data source lookup based on key fields
  • Local cache to improve lookup performance
  • Configurable timed cache data refresh

Derive geographic information such as country, city, region from IP fields by locally integrating the IP2Location database.

  • : Geographic fields to extract (e.g., country, city)

Mask sensitive information such as phone numbers, ID cards, emails, IP addresses, supporting various masking rules (mask, fuzzy replacement, etc.) to ensure privacy compliance.

The field that needs to be desensitized
Rule type: Positive desensitization, Equal desensitization
Desensitization type: Phone number, ID number, Email, IP address; Required when choosing positive desensitization
Equal content; Required when choosing equal desensitization
Display mode: Full desensitization, Head-tail desensitization, Middle desensitization

Common Masking Strategies:

  • Mask middle four digits of phone numbers:
  • Mask email account name:
  • Mask IP address:

7. Dictionary Translation

Convert encoded values into business semantics (e.g., gender code => , => ), improving data readability and report quality.

Field list, format: target field name = source field name
Dictionary field list. Each dictionary object has the following attributes:
Dictionary source, "FILE" means the dictionary is from a file, "CONFIG" means it is from a string.
Dictionary string, in JSONObject format. Required when type = "CONFIG".
Path to the dictionary file, content is in JSONObject format. Required when type = "FILE".
Delimiter for dictionary items in the dictionary file. Default is a comma.
  • Configured JSON format string data
  • Referenced TEXT files containing dictionary content

8. Extended Incremental Reading for SFTP/FTP

SeaTunnel natively supports reading remote files but has room for optimization in incremental pulling, breakpoint resume, and multithreaded consumption. We extended the following capabilities:

  • Incremental detection based on file modification time: automatically detect new or changed files
  • Thread pool scan triggering: schedule file pulling with timed tasks
  • Concurrent processing with multiple consumers and consumption modes: improve throughput and avoid duplicate consumption
  • Read offset record and breakpoint resume: ensure no data loss on retries
  • Log and health status monitoring: support real-time alerts and logging
  • Historical file cleanup strategy: automatically clean old data according to retention days

Performance Test (Real Data):

  • Data volume: 10 million records, target table fields: 72
  • Kafka throughput: 55,000 records/second

  • Environment configuration:

    • Kafka, SeaTunnel, ClickHouse all deployed on single machines
    • OS: CentOS 7, CPU: 8 cores 16 threads, Memory: 32G
    • SeaTunnel JVM:
Maximum Processing Rate (rows/s)
Ftp/Sftp Incremental Reading

Component Development Case Sharing

SeaTunnel’s data processing and transformation API defines the basic framework and behavior of transformation operations through abstract classes and interfaces. Specific transformation operation classes inherit and implement these abstractions to complete specific data transformation logic. This design grants SeaTunnel's transformation operations good extensibility and flexibility.

SeaTunnel Transform API Architecture Analysis

1. (Interface)

  • Role: Top-level interface for transform plugins, defines a unified entry for reading data from source and performing transformations.
    • : Abstract method to be implemented by subclasses, converts raw data into new structures.
    • getProducedCatalogTable(): Returns the structure information of the transformed data table, ensuring consistency with the data returned by .

2. AbstractSeaTunnelTransform (Abstract Class)

  • Role: Implements interface and encapsulates common logic.
  • Functionality: Unifies the handling process of , so subclasses only focus on specific transformation logic.

3. AbstractCatalogSupportTransform (Abstract Class)

  • Role: On top of the previous abstraction, further abstracts field mapping and data structure transformation.
  • Functionality: Provides unified field mapping and Catalog mapping support, facilitating integration with metadata systems.

4. Concrete classes like ,

  • Type: Concrete implementation classes
  • Role: Implement and methods, defining specific transformation logic for each data row and output structure.
  • Functionality: Represent user-customized transformation rules, such as regex parsing, field splitting, etc.

Based on Apache SeaTunnel’s efficient and extensible Transform mechanism, we developed the above components. Next, we analyze two cases to illustrate the new capabilities brought by these extensions.

Case 1: Regex Parsing Capability

  • Requirements and Background:

    • An internet company needs to ingest host login logs for analysis of abnormal user behaviors like brute force attacks, account theft, and borrowing.
    • Sample data: [2023-08-15 14:23:45 [ERROR] 192.168.1.1 - Login failed for user zhangsan].
    • Log features: complex format, multiple key information blended together, inconsistent format with no fixed field delimiters.
    • Solution: Build a regex-based log parsing function to dynamically identify and extract key information from irregular logs (log files, host logs, application logs), converting unstructured log content into structured data.
  1. Identify the upstream field to be parsed.
  2. Write regular expressions. Define regex patterns in config files using capture groups to mark key data segments to extract.
  3. Define mapping between result fields and regex capture groups (). SeaTunnel reads raw data and applies predefined regex line by line to match patterns, automatically extracting data for each capture group and mapping extracted results to pre-configured target fields.

Case 2: Dynamic Data Completion Capability

  • Requirements and Background:

    • A bank needs to ingest customer transaction data, but the raw data lacks key information such as transaction purpose and payee name, affecting subsequent risk monitoring and anti-money laundering analysis.
    • Sample data: Transaction ID: TX20250426001, Customer Account: 622202****1234, Transaction Time: 2024-09-10 08:00, Amount: 5,000 CNY, Channel: Mobile Banking.
    • Log features: missing transaction purpose, payee name, and other key information.
    • Solution: Build a function to complete missing data fields based on lookup auxiliary data streams, ensuring data integrity and consistency.
  1. Identify source fields and dimension (lookup) table fields.
  2. Configure auxiliary data source JDBC connection, driver, username, and password, supporting any JDBC-type database.
  3. Define custom SQL to complete data. The rowjoin component uses configured database connection to query the database and write SQL query results into a Caffeine cache. The cache expiry mechanism refreshes data by re-executing SQL when data expires.

Intelligent Fault Tolerance Mechanism for Dirty Data

In large-scale data processing tasks, a small amount of abnormal data should not cause the entire task to fail. We designed a closed-loop mechanism of “error classification ➡ detection ➡ handling ➡ recording” to ensure handling of all types of exceptions during massive data processing.

The principle is: individual dirty data or exceptions should not interrupt the entire task, while all error information is retained for subsequent fixing and auditing.

  • Do not interrupt main flow: Dirty data can be recorded and skipped without affecting overall task execution.

  • Hierarchical handling strategy:

    • Parsing exceptions (e.g., invalid JSON/XML format)
    • Mapping exceptions (field type mismatch, missing fields)
    • Network/IO exceptions (external source connection failures)

  • Full chain error logging and tracing: Include raw data, exception type, and handling result.

  • Configurable retry mechanism: Allow limited automatic retries for recoverable exceptions.

Future Plans and Evolution Directions

To make Apache SeaTunnel better meet our business scenarios, we will continue to evolve data processing capabilities along the following directions:

  1. JDBC-based time incremental: Use scheduled tasks and timestamp fields to query incremental data from databases, suitable for environments where database configurations cannot be modified.
  2. API incremental collection: Periodically call third-party business system APIs over HTTP/HTTPS to obtain the latest asset qualification data.
  3. Plan to expand collector plugins for Syslog protocol to capture network device and system logs in real-time, supporting high-availability HA and load balancing.
  4. More intelligent parsing: Explore use of NLP, AI-based parsing methods for complex text content.
  5. Build unified monitoring platform with error alarm, task status tracking, and consumption flow metrics.
  6. Continue adding connectors to support more big data, cloud, and enterprise databases.

In summary, secondary development of Apache SeaTunnel provides a powerful and flexible platform for addressing the highly complex, heterogeneous, and large-scale data integration challenges faced by enterprises in cybersecurity and finance. Through self-developed transformation components, advanced incremental reading strategies, and intelligent fault tolerance mechanisms, we have greatly improved the robustness, accuracy, and real-time performance of data pipelines.

SeaTunnel’s extensible architecture and modular plugin design allow us to quickly implement business-specific functions while benefiting from the rich ecosystem and ongoing community innovation. We hope this sharing will provide practical reference and inspiration for other developers and engineers working in complex enterprise data integration scenarios.

items.title: 5 Dev Trends You Can't Ignore in 2025
items.author: Mahadi Islam
items.content:
94% of organizations are adopting AI coding tools, but most aren’t ready for what comes next.

The software development landscape is shifting fast. What used to be a structured, sprint-driven process is now infused with real-time decisions, AI-assisted workflows, and rising pressure to deliver faster.

Here are the trends reshaping how teams build software in 2025—and what they mean in practice:

1. AI-Powered Development Tools (Copilot)

GitHub reports that developers using Copilot code , with 46% of their code now AI-generated. What was once seen as assistance is now a catalyst for acceleration. Teams that embrace AI-driven tools are not just speeding up their workflow—they’re enhancing code quality and productivity.

According to Puppet, 94% of organizations with a dedicated platform engineering team report improved development velocity. Internal developer platforms are now the productivity layer enabling faster, more efficient development cycles. Building and deploying software has never been easier.

3. Serverless & Multi-Cloud

AWS has seen a 40% year-over-year increase in Lambda usage. Serverless architecture enables teams to bypass infrastructure constraints and focus purely on business logic. This flexibility boosts deployment speed and ensures resilience, even under increased demand.

Gartner estimates that by 2026, 65% of all app development will be low-code. This doesn’t replace developers—it redirects their efforts to solving complex business problems. Low-code tools enable faster prototyping and scaling, allowing teams to concentrate on core logic and innovation.

GitLab’s 2023 survey shows that integrating security early in the software development lifecycle (SDLC) reduces remediation time by 50%. Security is no longer a post-development step—it's now embedded in the flow, helping teams deliver secure software without sacrificing speed.

These trends are reshaping how teams approach software development. AI tools, platform engineering, serverless solutions, low-code platforms, and embedded security are not just enhancing productivity—they’re accelerating innovation.

How is your team adapting to these shifts? Share your insights!

items.title: Why Should You Choose Renting Cloud GPU?
items.author: RunC.AI Offical
items.content:
Think about the times when you have an urgent deadline about an AI project or application development, no time for debugging and scalability is the key. What's next?

Nowadays, companies, universities, researchers, and individual developers have started to rent a GPU or entire GPU servers instead of buying or local deployment.

The truth is, without the long-term commitment or expenses needed, renting cloud GPUs is the most flexible and affordable solution when dealing with heavy processing power.

Renting GPUs provides flexible on demand model and do not require one-time huge hardware investment such as high-performance GPUs, supporting servers, and cooling equipment, etc.

GPU rental platform usually supports a variety of GPU models and users can adjust resource configuration any time. No limitations by hardware specifications.

items.title: The Role of an iOS App Development Company in Smart Cities
items.author: John William
items.content:
While AI is transforming cities across all platforms, the user experience and security standards on iOS devices are particularly important for citizen engagement. Partnering with an ensures that smart city apps are not only innovative but also user-friendly, secure, and compliant with Apple's strict privacy guidelines. iOS devices are widely used by urban residents, making this platform essential for reaching and engaging the community.

Overcoming Challenges in Building Smart Cities

1. Data Privacy and Security Smart city apps collect and process vast amounts of citizen data, making data privacy and security paramount. AI algorithms must be designed and implemented in a way that protects sensitive information and complies with privacy regulations.

2. Integration with Legacy Infrastructure Many cities rely on outdated infrastructure and legacy systems. Integrating AI solutions with these systems can be complex and require careful planning.

Smart city technologies should benefit all citizens, regardless of their socioeconomic status or background. It's important to address equity and inclusion issues and ensure that smart city initiatives are fair and accessible to everyone.

4. Public Trust and Acceptance Citizens need to trust that smart city technologies are being used responsibly and for their benefit. Transparency and open communication are essential for building public trust and acceptance.

The Future of AI in Smart Cities

The future of smart cities is intelligent, connected, and citizen-centric. We are already seeing the rise of AI-powered virtual assistants for city services, predictive policing systems, and personalized transportation solutions. As AI technology continues to advance, expect even more innovative applications, such as AI-driven urban planning, autonomous infrastructure maintenance, and real-time environmental monitoring.

Why Choose an AI App Development Company?

Working with an experienced gives cities and urban planners a competitive edge. From strategy and design to development and deployment, experts can help you unlock the full potential of AI, ensuring your smart city apps are scalable, secure, and citizen-friendly.

If you're ready to build a smarter, more sustainable, and more livable city with AI, now is the time to explore what's possible. is a leading AI app development company with deep expertise in creating innovative mobile solutions for urban environments. Contact us to learn how we can help you transform your city for the better.

items.title: From Chaos To Clarity: Making Your Data AI Ready
items.author: Marcus Wiens
items.content:
A gold rush causes people to miss their mark. I’ve often heard that when there is a gold rush happening, sell shovels, sifts, and maps. Today the equivalent is selling Large Language Models (LLMs) and AI-Agents. While these models and agents deliver on their promises, in order to work for any real world organization they need data, and not just any data, but clean, structured and well documented data.

Many organizations are discovering an uncomfortable truth: their data isn't ready for the AI revolution. Not unlike inviting a master chef to your kitchen only to realize you've stocked the pantry with expired ingredients and unlabeled spices. No matter how sophisticated your AI tools are, they can't create gourmet insights from spoiled data.

The Data Quality Crisis: Why Your AI Projects Are Struggling

Picture this: Your organization has invested millions in cutting-edge AI solutions. The executive team is eagerly awaiting the transformative insights promised in the sales pitch. But months later, your AI initiatives are stalling, producing unreliable results, or failing to launch entirely.

Sound familiar? You're not alone.

According to industry research, approximately 73% of AI projects struggle or fail completely, and poor data quality is often the primary culprit.

The Real Cost of Dirty Data

Bad data isn't just an inconvenience—it is expensive and not just in time and IT costs. When you compile the lost opportunity costs Organizations lose an estimated 15-25% of their revenue due to poor data quality, with further unquantifiable losses in opportunities and insights.

Slow and steady. The best way to adopt any technology is with a clear head. By rushing technology into data that isn’t ready, you risk damaging customer trust and losing your competitive edge.Make sure your organization understand clean objects and limitations of new technologies as you move forward.

The Journey to AI-Ready Data: A Roadmap

Step 1: Conduct a Data Quality Audit

Before diving into AI adoption, take stock of your current data landscape. Like a chef picking out quality ingredients during prep and before service, your data quality audit should examine:

  • : Are there missing values in critical fields?
  • : Does your data reflect reality?
  • : Does the same information appear differently across systems?
  • : How current is your data?
  • : Does your data actually connect to the business problems you're trying to solve?

Step 2: Establish Data Governance (The Fun Way!)

Think of it as establishing the "rules of the road" or “kitchen hierarchy” for your organization's most valuable asset. Effective governance includes:

  • : Appoint "Data Champions" across departments who advocate for quality,
  • : Define who is responsible for what data (no more "that is not my problem" syndrome),
  • : Create specific, measurable criteria for "good data,"
  • : Build a data dictionary that even non-technical staff can understand.
  • : Gamify your governance! Create friendly competition between departments for data quality improvements, with real rewards for teams that excel.

Step 3: Implement Automated Data Cleaning Processes

Manual data cleaning is like trying to empty the ocean with a teaspoon—technically possible but wildly impractical. Automation is essential:

  • : Set up guardrails that prevent bad data from entering your systems,
  • : Automatically format addresses, phone numbers, and other common fields,
  • : Identify and merge duplicate records, Outlier Detection: Flag statistically improbable values for human review,
  • : Automatically supplement internal data with trusted external sources.

Step 4: Create a Continuous Improvement Cycle

Data quality is not a one-time project, but an ongoing discipline. Establish:

  • : Monitor and share data quality scores across the organization,
  • : Make it easy for users to report data issues, Root Cause Analysis: Don't just fix errors—understand why they occurred,
  • : Ensure everyone understands their role in maintaining data quality.

Real-World Success Stories: Data Cleaning Hero

The most common problem we encounter is companies paying tens of thousands to hundreds of thousands of dollars a month to maintain and store data that they are not gaining anything from. By understanding what data your organization needs to collect versus what they are collecting you can start saving your company money.

From this money savings you can invest in data engineers and data scientists that can not only manage and organize data but can glean critical business insights that can multiply earnings and profits. One of our early customers, a major retail chain, discovered they had over 30% duplicate customer records in their database. After implementing an enterprise-wide data cleaning initiative, they not only eliminated the duplicates but also discovered $230,000 in yearly cost savings. These duplicates were later diagnosed as missed upselling opportunities by correctly streamlining duplicate customer purchase histories.

Getting Started: Your 30-Day Data Readiness Plan

Ready to prepare your data for AI success? Here is your first month's action plan:

Take our AI Readiness Questionnaire at aireadycompass.comEnter for your chance to win during our May 2025 draw!

Conclusion: Data Quality as Competitive Advantage

In the AI era, clean, well-structured data is nott just a technical requirement—it is a strategic asset that creates sustainable competitive advantage. Organizations that master data quality will see their AI investments pay dividends, while those that neglect it will continue to wonder why their competitors continue to gain market share.

: The most sophisticated AI in the world can't overcome the limitations of poor-quality data. Make data readiness the foundation of your AI strategy, and you'll be positioned for success in the intelligence revolution.

About the Author: Marcus is a data strategy consultant specializing in helping organizations prepare their data infrastructure for AI adoption.
