items.title: Setting Up Modular Capability Plugins (MCP) with Amazon Q on Arch Linux
items.author: Dulanjana Lakmal
items.content:
Hello everyone! Today I'm writing about one of the most viral topics in the developer world right now: Modular Capability Plugins (MCP).

MCPs (Modular Capability Plugins) are specialized extensions that enhance AI assistants like Amazon Q with specific capabilities. Think of MCPs as "super-powered tool belts" for AI assistants - they give these assistants specialized knowledge and abilities to perform specific tasks far better than they could with general training alone.

We can think like this if Amazon Q is like a smartphone out of the box, MCPs are like specialized apps you install to transform it from a general-purpose device into a professional-grade tool for specific tasks. Just as you might install Photoshop for image editing or Final Cut Pro for video production, MCPs add specialized capabilities to your AI assistant.

My Experience with Amazon Q MCPs For the past month, I've been using Amazon Q Chat service for my day-to-day DevOps and CloudOps tasks. It's been an incredibly helpful tool that streamlines my workflow. Since I'm running Arch Linux on my personal laptop, I decided to set up several specialized MCPs to enhance my productivity.

As a cloud engineer, I frequently encounter several time-consuming tasks:

  • AWS architecture diagrams have always been a critical but manual and time-consuming task for developers and cloud architects
  • Searching through AWS documentation efficiently can be challenging
  • Following Terraform best practices and security-first development workflows requires constant reference checking

The Solution: Custom MCPs To address these challenges, I've set up the following MCPs:

awslabs.aws-diagram-mcp-server *- For generating AWS architecture diagrams automatically **awslabs.aws-documentation-mcp-server *- For searching AWS documentation using the official AWS search API, getting content recommendations, and converting documentation to markdown format **awslabs.terraform-mcp-server - For AWS Terraform best practices, security-first development workflows, Checkov integration, AWS provider documentation, AWS-IA GenAI modules, and Terraform workflow execution

Setup Guide for Arch Linux Prerequisites Before beginning, make sure you have:

  • python-uv (Python packaging tool)
  • AWS CLI configured locally with an AWS Builder ID for accessing Amazon Q

Install Amazon Q on Arch Linux

curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf "https://desktop-release.q.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/latest/q-x86_64-linux.zip" -o "q.zip"
unzip q.zip
./q/install.sh

After installation, you should see an "amazonq" folder under your .aws folder.

Create an MCP configuration file:

vim ~/.aws/amazonq/mcp.json

Add the following content to configure all three MCP servers:

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "awslabs.terraform-mcp-server": {
      "command": "uvx",
      "args": ["awslabs.terraform-mcp-server@latest"],
      "env": {
        "FASTMCP_LOG_LEVEL": "ERROR"
      },
      "disabled": false,
      "autoApprove": []
    },
   "awslabs.aws-documentation-mcp-server": {
        "command": "uvx",
        "args": ["awslabs.aws-documentation-mcp-server@latest"],
        "env": {
          "FASTMCP_LOG_LEVEL": "ERROR"
        },
        "disabled": false,
        "autoApprove": []
    },
   "awslabs.aws-diagram-mcp-server": {
      "command": "uvx",
      "args": ["awslabs.aws-diagram-mcp-server"],
      "env": {
        "FASTMCP_LOG_LEVEL": "ERROR"
      },
      "autoApprove": [],
      "disabled": false
    }
  }
}

Note: If you don't need all three MCPs, you can remove the ones you don't want from your mcp.json file.

Open Amazon Q - you should see it initializing the MCP servers Enter /tools in the chat to check what tools you have access to via Amazon Q MCPs

If you encounter any issues, double-check your Python version, the uv package installation, and that GraphViz is properly installed

To verify everything is working correctly, try a sample prompt like:

generate an aws architecture diagram for sample s3 bucket with cloudfront for hosting static website

This should trigger the diagram MCP to create a visualization of the architecture. It should produce an output similar to the one below:

This is the end of my post. Thank you for reading through it. If you liked it, please consider sharing it with your colleagues for better reach.

items.title: The Role of Prior Art in Invalidating a Patent: Key Concepts
items.author: Muhammad Abdullah
items.content:
In the high-stakes world of intellectual property, a single overlooked document can spell the difference between a granted patent and a successful invalidation. For patent attorneys, understanding how to leverage prior art effectively is more than just due diligence — it's a strategic imperative. This article explores a powerful truth in patent litigation: how strong prior art can lead to successful patent invalidation.

Prior art — encompassing any evidence that an invention was already known — is often the cornerstone of invalidation efforts. Whether you're challenging a competitor’s patent or defending your client's innovation, knowing how to uncover, analyze, and apply prior art can dramatically shift the odds in your favor.

In this comprehensive guide, we’ll explore the role of prior art in invalidating a patent, breaking down key legal concepts, best practices for searching and evaluating prior art, and the procedural pathways available through mechanisms like IPR and PGR. We’ll also walk through a detailed case study illustrating how strong prior art led to a decisive invalidation, providing valuable lessons for attorneys in the field.

What Qualifies as Prior Art?

Prior art includes any publicly available information that predates a patent’s filing. This includes:

  • Existing patents and applications

Example: In Apple Inc. v. Samsung Electronics, Samsung used prior art to invalidate some of Apple’s design patents — showcasing how strategic discovery can tilt the scales in litigation.

Types of Prior Art: Patent vs. Non-Patent Literature

  • : Published patents, applications, and patent family data from USPTO, EPO, etc.
  • Non-Patent Literature (NPL): Journals, white papers, product guides, trade show posters — often overlooked, yet invaluable.

Global Differences in Prior Art Standards

  • : Any public disclosure before filing is fair game.
  • : Similar rules, but tighter on what qualifies as a disclosure.
  • : Nuanced rules around disclosure and grace periods.

The Legal Framework of Patent Invalidation

Key Grounds for Invalidation

    Legal Instruments: IPR, PGR, and Opposition

    • Inter Partes Review (IPR)

    Jurisdictional Variations

    Always verify local rules: What invalidates in the U.S. may not hold in Europe or Asia.

    How Prior Art Undermines Patent Validity

    If a single prior art reference discloses every feature of a claim, it is anticipated and invalid.

    Combinations of multiple references can lead to rejection if the invention is deemed a logical step forward.

    If the prior art or patent fails to teach how to make or use the invention, it may be disqualified.

    Search Strategies Used by Professionals

    • Boolean and semantic search
    • Forward/backward referencing

    AI-based tools can uncover non-obvious prior art quickly, even from fringe publications or foreign filings.

    Evaluating the Strength of Prior Art

    Claim Charting Techniques

    Create tables comparing each element of a claim to the corresponding feature in a prior art reference.

    The art must be publicly available before the effective filing date of the challenged patent.

    Relevance and Jurisdiction

    Ensure the cited prior art meets regional standards of admissibility.

    Case Study: How Strong Prior Art Led to Successful Patent Invalidation

    A niche patent in the pool-cleaning robot space was challenged by identifying an old user manual published 18 months before the filing.

    • Searched through consumer manuals and archived websites.
    • Built a detailed claim chart matching the manual with the patent claims.

    The PTAB ruled the patent invalid for both anticipation and obviousness.

    Common Mistakes in Prior Art-Based Invalidation

    • Relying on a single jurisdiction
    • Inadequate claim interpretation

    Strategic Considerations for Attorneys

    • Conduct prior art searches early
    • Balance cost vs. strategic advantage when launching an IPR or PGR

    The Future of Prior Art Searching

    • Predictive search using machine learning
    • Crowdsourced data tagging

    Newer platforms combine search, analytics, and claim charting in one dashboard — streamlining workflows.

    Understanding and leveraging strong prior art is not just an advantage — it’s a necessity. As we’ve explored, it can directly challenge a patent’s novelty or non-obviousness, leading to a successful invalidation.

    From search strategy to legal execution, the ability to uncover compelling prior art separates strategic patent attorneys from the rest.

    Need help strengthening your invalidity strategy? Start by reviewing our Software vs. Service article or partnering with expert search specialists.

    • Prior art is the foundation of any patent invalidation strategy.
    • Both patent literature and non-patent literature are vital sources.
    • Strong prior art supports challenges on novelty, obviousness, or enablement.
    • Claim charting is essential to present clear legal arguments.
    • Attorneys must understand global differences in prior art standards.
    • Even obscure manuals or industry whitepapers can lead to invalidation.
    • AI tools and search automation are transforming how prior art is found.

    1. What qualifies as strong prior art in patent invalidation?

    Any public disclosure before the filing date that matches the claimed invention.

    2. How do attorneys find prior art for patent invalidation cases?

    Through patent databases, NPL searches, and automated platforms.

    3. What is the role of claim charting in evaluating prior art?

    It visually matches each claim element to prior art, supporting invalidation.

    4. Can non-patent literature be used to invalidate a patent?

    Absolutely — as long as it's publicly available and relevant.

    5. What are common mistakes in a prior art search?

    Ignoring NPL, misinterpreting claims, and using U.S.-centric standards globally.

    Thanks for reading — we hope this deep dive offered valuable insights for your legal practice.

    Share this article on LinkedIn or with your team.

    What’s your biggest hurdle when conducting a prior art search?

    Drop your thoughts below — let’s start the conversation.
